title: gagea lutea var. transsilvanica
intro: Gagea lutea var. transsilvanica, also known as the yellow star of Bethlehem, is a flowering plant species within the lily family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its expectorant and diuretic effects. The plant has been associated with various health benefits, including the relief of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve urinary function. Gagea lutea var. transsilvanica can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is often used in herbal medicine to support overall health and well-being.
